Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
Laboratory results are largely within normal limits except for elevated NT-proBNP, mildly increased LDL cholesterol, and low HDL cholesterol. The elevated NT-proBNP may indicate possible cardiac dysfunction or heart failure and warrants clinical correlation. Lipid profile suggests increased cardiovascular risk. No evidence of anemia, infection, renal, or hepatic dysfunction. Recommend further cardiac evaluation and lipid management as clinically indicated.
Provider Organization: St Matthews Health & Community Centre
